identity.name: The Miller Who Never Paid
identity.kind: miller, retired (the debt outlived the mill)
identity.story: Shorted a piper's fee once, in a town two tales over — these things get paid for eventually, whether or not you're the one who owes them. Settled in Fableton and found only one piper left to pay: a cousin of that one, working strictly rats, strictly contracted. Neither has ever agreed whose debt it actually is, and neither has ever let that stop the payments.

relationships.kind: pays down a fee that was never his cousin's to begin with, one short coin at a time; found the original slip folded under the millstone's foot this lamplighting, the number written in a hand that wasn't his own and smaller than every coin he's carried to the stall since he settled here — folded it back under exactly where it was and decided, again, to carry the difference anyway, said to no one

behavior.label: the Miller's debt that isn't his

child.label: grinding a morning's flour, short the same coin as always
paying down what was never quite his to owe

children.label: crossing to where the lesser piper keeps his rounds
handing over a coin the piper cousin didn't ask for and won't refuse

child.label: sweeping up a debt that was never quite counted right

children.label: sweeping the mill floor same as every lamplighting, keeping better count than he ever did the first time
the broom catching a fold of paper wedged under the millstone's foot — the original fee slip from the town two tales over, kept without ever meaning to
reading a number in a hand that was never his own, plainer than he's let himself read it in years, and finding it smaller than every coin he's carried to the piper's stall since he settled here
folding the slip back under the millstone's foot exactly where it was, and deciding, again, to carry the difference anyway — the piper never once asked him to

child.label: sleeping easy, one coin lighter than a debt that isn't his
